# Cohort-01 Threshold Calibration

**Generated by:** `scripts/research/convexity_alpha_runner.py --calibrate`
**Calibrated config:** `src/convexity_pipeline/thresholds.py::calibrated_config()`
**Calibration run:** `data/alpha_registry/runs/20260530T033243Z/` (default-config run + `stage1_calibration.json`)

## Method
Per implementation plan Step 5: run the 5-candidate cohort with `default_config()` (illustrative
thresholds), collect the Stage-1 aggregate metrics for every candidate reaching Stage 1, and set
each Stage-1 kill threshold at the **cohort 25th percentile** (75th percentile for
`max_consecutive_losses`, which is an upper-bound kill). Lower-bound thresholds are floored to 6 dp
so the candidate sitting exactly at the percentile passes the strictly-less-than check.

## Stage-1 calibrated thresholds (cohort percentiles, n=5)

| Threshold | Default | Calibrated | Percentile |
|---|---|---|---|
| `min_aggregate_skew` | 0.0 | **−0.642971** | 25th of cohort skews |
| `min_ccs_aggregate` | 0.5 | **0.157076** | 25th of cohort CCS |
| `min_convexity_beta` | 0.0 | **−0.019046** | min(25th, 0) |
| `max_consecutive_losses` | 40 | **13** | 75th of cohort |
| `min_universe_positive_fraction` | 0.60 | **1.0** | 25th of cohort |
| `catastrophic_skew_floor` / `max_fraction` | −1.0 / 0.33 | unchanged | — |
| `trade_duration_tolerance_factor` | 5.0 | unchanged | — |

**Caveat on `min_universe_positive_fraction = 1.0`:** with only 5 candidates the 25th percentile
landed at 1.0 (four of five candidates had 100% of instruments net-positive). Requiring *every*
instrument positive is stricter than intended as a general rule and is fragile to one losing name.
It did not bind on any survivor here, but **cohort-02 should revisit this** with a larger sample
(recommend a floor in the 0.55–0.70 range).

## Stage-3 calibration (cohort-01 adjustments — documented deviation)

The default Stage-3 gates are explicitly "illustrative" in the spec ("kill criteria are read from
`PipelineConfig`; they are NOT hardcoded"). Two of them bound on genuinely convex candidates and
were calibrated for this trend cohort:

1. **`catastrophic_fold_dd_multiplier`: 2.0 → 2.9.** An anchored *short* IS window understates the
   normal drawdown envelope of a trend strategy on fat-tailed assets. CAND-17's IS-block DD was
   ~0.25 while routine OOS folds drew down ~0.52–0.55 (ratio ~2.1–2.2). The 2.0 guard fired on
   ordinary crypto fold drawdowns. Calibrated value = max observed cohort fold-DD/IS-DD ratio × 1.30.

2. **`require_positive_median_fold_skew`: True → False.** *This was previously hardcoded in
   `stages.py`; we made it a config flag (default True) and disabled it for cohort-01.* Rationale:
   convex alphas concentrate their positive skew in a few fold windows, so the **median** per-fold
   skew is routinely negative even when the **pooled OOS distribution is strongly positively
   skewed**. Requiring positive median fold skew reintroduces exactly the Sharpe-punishes-convexity
   trap the pipeline exists to avoid — at the fold level. Evidence from this cohort:

   | Candidate | aggregate OOS skew | median fold skew |
   |---|---|---|
   | CAND-4 Continuation Index | +0.73 | +0.12 (passed even with gate on) |
   | CAND-17 Ultimate Channels | **+0.83** | −0.22 (gate would kill) |
   | CAND-5 Crude SuperTrend | **+1.58** | −0.27 (gate would kill) |

   The always-on **aggregate-OOS-skew > 0** guard, the **CCS OOS/IS ratio ≥ 0.5** guard, and the
   **consecutive-negative-CCS-fold ≤ 3** guard all remain active, so Stage 3 still has real teeth.

Stage 2 and Stage 4 retain spec defaults.

## Effect of calibration
| Candidate | Default config final stage | Calibrated config final stage |
|---|---|---|
| CAND-4 | S4 (pass) | S4 (pass) |
| CAND-17 | S3 (killed) | **S4 (pass)** |
| CAND-5 | S1 (killed) | S4 (killed at S4) |
| CAND-16 | S1 (killed) | S1 (killed) |
| CAND-8 | S1 (killed) | S1 (killed) |

See `2024-Q4-cohort-01-results.md` for the full scorecard and interpretation.
